title: The New Year: A time to Know Jesus
theme: There is nothing you can do this year that is more important than getting to know Jesus and trusting Him as your Savior.
object: Two sets of resolutions on strips of paper in two separate brown paper bags.
body:
These verses in the Bible talk about a time for doing different things.

Who can tell me what special day we celebrated a few days ago?  (New Year's)

New Year's is a time when people make New Year's resolutions.

Who can tell me what a resolution is?

It's a promise you make to yourself to do something better this year than last.

What do you think are some of the resolutions that people make?

Probably the most common resolution is to lose weight.

A lot of people make the resolution to go to the gym to exercise and get strong.

Maybe some people have a bad habit they need to give up.

Have any of you make your New Year's resolutions yet?

Well I have some New Year's resolutions here in this bag, and I'm going to let you take one today.

See if you don't think they're good promises to keep this year.

[Pass out and read resolutions on improved behavior.]

If you keep all of these resolutions, my won't your parents be surprised!

But, you know, I have another bag of resolutions that I thank are even more important.

If you learn to live by these resolutions, your life will be a real success.

[Pass out and read resolutions on trusting Jesus.]

So what will your New Year's resolution be?

I hope that this year you'll believe in Jesus more than ever before.

I hope this year you'll trust Him as the one who died for you and forgives your sins.

I hope this year you'll let Him be your special Friend.

Yes, there is a time for everything and a season for everything under heaven.

Now is the time to make Jesus your Savior and Friend.

How many want to make Jesus their best friend this year?

Behavioral Resolutions:

I will get up in the morning the first time I'm called.

I will get up in the morning without complaining.

I will go to bed without complaining.

I will obey my parents and grandparents.

I will fill up on good food instead of junk.

I will make my bed each day.

I will keep my room neat and clean.

I will remember to say please and thank you.

I will obey my teachers and do my best in school.

I will be quiet and listen in church.

Faith Resolutions:

I will talk each day to my friend, Jesus.

I will forgive others because Jesus forgives me.

I will tell my friends that Jesus loves them.

I will listen to the Bible and Bible stories so I know Jesus better.

I will come to church and Sabbath School to learn more about my friend Jesus.

I will believe God's promises in the Bible.

I will be thankful for the good things God has given me.

I will love Jesus for dying for my sins.

I will obey Jesus because I love Him.

I will ask Jesus to forgive my sins each day.

title: Listen and learn
theme: In order to learn we must listen carefully to what our teachers tell us.
object: Conch (Konk) shell to listen to the roar of the ocean.  Coupon books to hand out.
body:
This verse in the Bible tells us about something very important: faith.

Faith is believing in Jesus and trusting Him as a living person for the forgiveness of sins and for eternal life with God.

And this verse tells us how we can get faith: It comes by hearing what the Bible says about Jesus.

So listening and hearing are very important: In order to learn, you have to listen and hear.

Are you listening to me?  Are you learning what I am teaching you now?

title: Super Bowl Sunday
theme: Be a fan for Jesus if you want to be a winner in the end.
object: Patriot paraphernalia and pictures of fans
body:
That's an exciting verse isn't it?

It says that someday, all the governments of this world are going to pass away, and Jesus is going to be the King of the whole world forever and ever!!!  How exciting!!

Speaking of exciting things, there's a lot of people in NE that are excited today.  Do you know why?

Because tomorrow is the Super Bowl and the NE Patriots are playing the Seattle Sea Hawks to see which team will be the world champion in professional football.

Now people that follow football and root for a specific team like the Patriots are called fans.

A football fan is a football fanatic.  He's a fanatical supporter of his team.

A Patriot fan roots for his Patriots not matter what the score is or no matter how good the other team is.

Now I'm a Patriots fan.  And to prove it I have brought some Patriots paraphernalia with me this morning.  [Patriots pajamas, Tee-shirt, sweat shirt, blanket]

You can be sure that tomorrow night I will be sitting in front of the TV rooting for the Patriots to win.

But I'm not as fanatical as some of the people who dress up and go to the games.  Check out these pics!

Now the Super Bowl is fun to watch, and it's fun to root for our team.

But next year there will be another Super Bowl, probably with different teams.

And 50 years from now, very few people will remember who won this game, and very few people will care.

So what I want to say to you this morning is that there is something much more important than being a football fan: It's being a Jesus fan.

So I'm going to get out my Jesus Saves tee-shirt and put this seal on my forehead that says Jesus is Lord.

Did you know that Jesus is fighting a war for us.  It's a war against sin and Satan and evil and death.

And to win this war, Jesus had to die on the cross for our sins.

He did that because He loves us so much, and He wants everybody to be a Jesus fan.

Someday Jesus is coming back and He's going to save us and give us eternal life.

People might forget who wins the Super Bowl tomorrow, but nobody is every going to forget what Jesus did to save them.

When Jesus is King of all the earth, we're going to praise Him every Sabbath and thank Him that He won the victory for us.

How many would like to be a Jesus fan starting right now?

00Jesus is Lord

Jesus is Lord

title: God's Valentine
theme: God's love letter to us is the Bible
object: Valentine Card (attached), Candy Kiss, Heart-shaped candy box with NT inside.
body:
Did any of you celebrate Valentine's Day yesterday?

Who can tell me what Valentine's Day is all about? (Love and friendship)

Did you give any of your friends a valentine?  Did you get any from them?

How many got some candy on Valentine's Day?

How many like candy?

If you're quiet and listen to the children's sermon, I might have something sweet to share with you at the end.

It's nice to be kind to others and be friends with them.

That's what our verse of scripture says: "The greatest of these is love." [read again]

Love is greater than faith and hope.

That makes love the greatest thing in the world!

The greatest love in all the world is the love of God for you and me.

The Bible says that God loved us so much that He gave us His Son, Jesus, to die for our sins.

But you know, we wouldn't know about the love of God unless He sent us a "valentine" to tell us.

And here it is.  This is God's valentine to you and me.  [Show candy box.  Open it.  Get out the NT]

This is God's love letter to you and me.

In this little Bible God tells us that He is love, and He loves us with an amazing love.

Here let me read it to you. [Read John 3:16]

God loved you so much He sent Jesus to die for our sins so we can be forgiven and live with Him forever.

So I made you a valentine to tell you about God's love for you.  [Pass out Valentines.]

On the front it says Happy Valentine's Day.

So you might think this is a regular valentine that I might send to someone that I love.

But when you open it up, it tells about God's love for you. [Read and comment on valentine message]

That's real love!!

How many are interested in that kind of love?

Let's tell Jesus that we love Him too, and want to be His friends forever!

Prayer:

Pass out Hersey Kisses

title: St. Patrick and His Enemies
theme: Jesus forgave His enemies, so did St. Patrick, so should we.
object: St. Patrick Day paraphernalia or pictures of such.
body:
Who do you think prayed that prayer?  (Repeat prayer)

That was Jesus!  And the people He was asking God to forgive were the very ones that were nailing Him to the cross.  Jesus still loved them even though they were killing Him.

Today I want to tell you about someone else who was willing to forgive his enemies.

And it has to do with St. Patrick's day, a day we celebrate tomorrow.

[[Well we're almost to another holiday that comes on the 17th of March (Tuesday).

Who knows what Saint Patrick's Day is all about?

What color do you see on St. Patrick's Day?

Today it has become a day in which the Irish celebrate their country, Ireland.  (Others celebrate too.)

[Show pictures or objects] So these are the kinds of things you see on St. Patrick's Day: Pictures of Leprechauns or people dressed up like Leprechauns. Leprechauns are make-believe fairies or elves that spend their time making shoes and have a hidden pot of gold at the end of the rainbow. You see they have a shillelagh stick, and wear a Leprechaun hat.  And people dress up like that and march in a St. Patrick's-day parade.  And they carry Shamrocks which are three-leaf clovers that grows like grass.  And people eat traditional Irish food like corned beef and cabbage, [or the famous seven course Irish dinner: a six pack and a baked potato!]  And everybody wears green.

None of these have anything to do with St. Patrick.

St. Patrick was a Christian missionary to Ireland.

He was not even Irish himself: He was from Scotland.

When he was only 16 years old, he was kidnapped by slave traders and taken to Ireland where he was sold as a slave.

There Patrick worked on a farm.  He had to work very hard and do all the worst jobs.  Life was very hard.

But Patrick believed in God, so he began to pray, and he would pray all the time.

After he had been a slave for 6 long years, one night as he prayed, he heard a voice that told him his ship was waiting for him.

He believed that it was God's voice he heard.

So the next day, Patrick ran away and ran and walked 200 miles to the coast of Ireland.

There, just as God had told him, there was a ship, and it took him back to his home country of Scotland.

The Christians in Scotland at that time were called Celtic Christians.

These Christians believed the Bible, and because they went by the Bible, they kept the 7th day Sabbath just like our church does.

So Patrick was a Sabbath keeper.  He loved the Lord and wanted to become a missionary.

One night he had a dream where an angel had a lot of letters and he gave one to Patrick.

The letter told him that the Irish people wanted him to teach them about Jesus.

So Patrick became a missionary to Ireland, the same country where he had been a slave for 6 years.

Now you would think that Ireland was the last place Patrick would want to be a missionary.

But Patrick was able to forgive those who treated him mean, and he did something very nice for them.

He told them about Jesus who died for their sins and how they could be forgiven by trusting Him.

Like Jesus who forgave his enemies who nailed him to the cross, Patrick forgave his enemies who made him a slave.

So when I think of Saint Patrick's Day, I think of a man who forgave his enemies, who taught them about Jesus, who followed the Bible, and who kept the Sabbath.

I know that Jesus wants us to be forgiving to those who have been mean to us.

Do you know, you can forgive your enemies too, like Jesus and St. Patrick?

Let's ask Jesus to help us to be forgiving Christians that can love even our enemies.

title: He's Alive!!!
theme: Although He died on a cross, Jesus is alive forevermore.
object: Three crosses with three candles, the center one a trick candle that relights.
body:
Who knows what makes this weekend special?

Yes, it's Easter!  And on Easter we remember how Jesus died and rose again.

Nearly 2,000 years ago, Jesus was crucified on the cross on Friday, He was buried in the tomb on Sabbath, and He came back to life again on Sunday morning.

When Jesus came back to life, it was called a "resurrection."

So instead of calling Sunday "Easter," I like to call it Resurrection Day.

Because, for Christians, it's not so much about Easter bunnies, or Easter eggs, or Easter candy or Easter chickens, It's about Jesus defeating death.

So today I want to remember with you what the Bible says happened to Jesus when He died, was buried, and came back to life in the resurrection.

The Bible says there were 3 crosses on Calvary, so I have three crosses here today.

Let's think about the three men who were crucified on those three crosses.

The Bible tells us that Jesus was crucified between two thieves.

Jesus, who had never done anything wrong, was crucified between two men who had done all kinds of things wrong.

Let's let this candle represent the first thief.  And because he's alive, I'm going to light this candle.

There.  He's alive and hanging on his cross.

This thief didn't believe in Jesus.  He didn't believe that Jesus was the Son of God.  He didn't believe that Jesus could forgive his sins.  He didn't believe that Jesus could come back from the dead.

This thief died.  [Take out candle and blow out.]  He was buried.  He is dead today.  He has no hope.

Let's let this candle represent the second thief.  Of course, he was alive, so we'll light him up too.  [Light]

At first he made fun of Jesus, but he soon saw that Jesus was no ordinary man.

Before he died, this thief admitted that he was a sinner.

He believed that Jesus was the Son of God, and would rise from the dead.

And he trusted Jesus as his Savior.

The second thief died [blow out candle] and was buried.

But he died with hope because Jesus promised him that someday he would be in heaven with Jesus.

Both of the thieves died and they are still dead today.

But you know there was something different about the man on the center cross.  [Light trick candle]

He was no ordinary man.  He was God's Son in human flesh.

If my camera here was good enough you would see that this candle is red and white striped.

What do you think the red stands for?  If you said the blood of Jesus, you would be right.  Now what made Jesus bleed?  [tell about nails thorns and scourging.]

What do you think the white in the candle represents?  If you say the goodness of Jesus, you would be right.

He was put to death, not for His own sins, but for ours.

He paid the punishment for our sins by dying on the cross for them.

Because He died for our sins, we can be forgiven and live forever with Jesus.

All we have to do is tell Him we're sorry for our sins and ask Him to forgive us.

And if we trust Him and love Him, He will forgive us when we ask.

But like the other men on the cross, Jesus died too.  [Blow out trick candle.]

But now I want you to watch very closely.

Although He died and was buried in a tomb, on Sunday morning [wait for candle to light] He came back to life.

This is the difference: [hold up lit candle and extinguished candle]

Jesus is alive!  He is not in the grave somewhere.  He is in heaven right now.  He is risen.

And because He lives, when He comes back, He can give us eternal life too.

Here's the thief that believed.  When Jesus comes back, Jesus will give him life again.

Not only the thief.  But everyone who loves Jesus and has been forgiven will get eternal life.

How many would like Jesus to light them up when He returns?

title: Surprised to See Jesus
theme: We can see Jesus if we look in the right place.
object: Ink blot image of Jesus
body:
This is the climax of an amazing story that Luke tells us about Jesus after He came back from the dead.

Let me tell you what happened…

Here Jesus walked with them and talked with them for hours and they didn't recognize who He was.

But when Jesus said the blessing for their meal, they closed their eyes to pray, and all of a sudden, their eyes were opened and they saw it was Jesus.

They were so surprised that it was Jesus, but then He vanished from their sight.

Would you like to do an experiment to see if we can see what the disciples saw?

[Hand out ink blot images.]

Look at this ink blot on this piece of paper you are holding.  Do you think that looks like Jesus?

If you saw somebody that looked like that nobody would say, "O that looks like Jesus."

So this is like what the disciples saw when Jesus was walking with them on the road to Emmaus.

Now we are going to stare at these four little black dots in the middle for 15 seconds.  [Give instructions]

Then we are going to close our eyes like the disciples did when Jesus said the blessing.

And when you close your eyes, tell me what you see with your eyes closed.

The disciples were so excited that Jesus was alive and they actually talked with Him.

They ran back to Jerusalem to tell the other disciples that Jesus was alive!!!

Maybe it was like this ink blot for the disciples.

When they looked at Jesus, they didn't recognize Him, but when they closed their eyes for prayer, they saw Him.

And then what happened? He vanished, just like the image does for us after we see it for a few seconds!

Jesus is alive.  And when we close our eyes and pray, we can talk to Him just as though He were right at our side.

Hey, Let's pray right now and thank Jesus for dying for our sins and coming back to life again!

title: Candy Cane Bible Promise
theme: All the really good gifts come from God and they come attached to Jesus.
object: Candy Cane with Bible verse attached describing a gift from Jesus.
body:
This is another verse in the Bible that tells us about how God gives good things to us.

All the really good gifts come from God.

Last week we talked about the greatest gift ever given. Remember?

For God so loved the world that He gave His only begotten Son to die on the cross for our sins, that  whosoever believes in Him should not perish but have everlasting life.

There's no greater gift that has ever been given than the gift of Jesus.

But this verse in the Bible says that, even though God has given us the greatest gift already, He's not done giving yet.

God has more gifts for us, and they all come to us through Jesus.

The verse that we read says that if God already gave us the greatest gift in Jesus, we can depend on Him to freely give us all that He wants us to have if we accept Jesus as our Savior.

All the really good gifts come from God, and they come through Jesus our Savior.

Now there is something very special that we see only at Christmas time that reminds me of Jesus.

And I brought it in my bag today.  [Get out candy cane.]

It's a candy cane!  Can you think of any ways this candy cane can remind us of Jesus?

It's sweet.  It's refreshing.  It's good.  Just like Jesus: He's sweet.  He's refreshing.  He's good.

It's a cane.  People lean on a cane in order to walk straight.  We can lean on Jesus and He can help us to walk straight and make good decisions in life.

It's a shepherd's staff.  Jesus is the Good Shepherd that lays down His life for the sheep.

If you turn it upside down, what letter is it? "J".  And J is the first letter in the name Jesus.

It's colored red and white.  What does the red remind you of?  The blood that Jesus shed so we could be forgiven.

And white in the Bible always represents purity.  Jesus is the only one who never sinned.  He was good and pure and holy his whole life.

So when we see a candy cane, there are a lot of things that can remind us of Jesus.

Now as I look in my bag this morning, I can see some more candy canes.

These candy canes are special for two reasons: first they were bought by Pat Gosselin because she wanted to give a candy cane to each of you.

And second, they are special because they each come with a little card attached that tells you about one of the gifts Jesus gives to you.

Let me read them…

[Read the gifts indicated on each card.  Put them all back in the bag.]

After we pray, you can take a candy cane out of the bag.

But remember this: All the really good gifts come from God, and they come attached to Jesus.

The only way we can receive these gifts is to receive Jesus as our Savior and Lord.

Do you want to receive Jesus today?  Let's do it!  Let's invite Him into our hearts again!

title: The Unopened Gift
theme: God wants us to accept, use, and enjoy His great gift of salvation.
object: An unopened Christmas gift (A jar of honey from Eric and Carry)
body:
Good morning kids!  Did you all have a nice Christmas?

Did anybody get sticks or coal in their stocking?  Did anybody get a rotten potato?

You mean you all got nice presents?  You must have been good kids!!

Did you get anything for Christmas that you really liked?  What was one of your favorite gifts?

Well it sounds like everyone had a really nice Christmas, but it's all over for another year (364 days).

But guess what.  I have a Christmas present here that I haven't opened yet. [Get it out.]

How many of you have Christmas presents at home that you haven't opened yet?

You mean you opened them all right away?  You didn't save any for later?

You didn't put any in the closet to save until you were a teenager so you could open then?

Well, I'm glad you haven't saved your gifts, because when someone gives you a gift, they want you to open it, and use it, and enjoy it.

But this morning, this unopened gift reminds me that there are millions of people who haven't opened the best Christmas gift of all.

You see, Christmas is a time for us to remember the greatest gift every given.

God gave his own dear Son Jesus to come and die on the cross for our sins so we could be forgiven and be God's children forever.

The best gift ever didn't come wrapped in pretty paper and laid under a tree.

The best gift was God's Son, wrapped in human flesh and laid in a lowly manger.

And while many people try to make the Christmas tree the center of Christmas, the tree that really matters is the Old Rugged Cross, where Jesus stretched out His arms in love and died for you and me.

This is the greatest gift ever given: the gift of salvation through Jesus Christ our Lord.

God offers this gift to you and to me and to everyone in the world.

Anyone can turn on their radio, TV, or computer and hear the gospel, the good news about Jesus.

Anyone can pick up a bible and read the good news for themselves.

But you know what?  Most people aren't even interested enough to open the gift.

It's like they find this present under the tree, and they read the tag and it has their name on it, and it says:  "From God with all my love."

And they say, "O… It's from God…so what…big deal.  Maybe I'll open it someday…maybe just before I die… but right now, I'm not really interested in a present from God.

And they put it in the closet.

Would you treat God's greatest gift that way?  (No way!)

God wants you to receive it, to use it, and to enjoy it.

And the promise of God's word is that if you receive Jesus you will become a child of God.

Does that sound good?  Are your interested?  How many want to be a child of God?  Let's receive God's greatest gift!  [prayer]

Hey, I wonder what's in this gift.  You want to open it?  Maybe it's something we can use and enjoy.

[Have spoons so each can have a taste of honey.]

title: Butterfly or Caterpillar
theme: Only God can change us from sinners to saints.
object: Picture of Monarch butterfly and caterpillar
body:
God is amazing!  God can do great things!

The more we learn about the things God has created, the more we can appreciate how great God is.

There are many beautiful and amazing things that God has made, but the one I want to tell you about today is the butterfly.

After pot luck today, we're going to watch a DVD about butterflies.  It's really good.  Don't miss it!

So for the children's sermon, I want to tell you about butterflies and how they change.

Here's a picture of a Monarch Butterfly.  Isn't it beautiful?  [Describe]

But look how the butterfly starts out.

Here's a picture of the Monarch butterfly caterpillar.  Isn't it ugly? [Describe]

So how does this ugly caterpillar turn into this beautiful butterfly?

It does so by following the instructions that God placed in its genetic code.

The caterpillar forms a chrysalis in which it breaks down into an organic soup of different molecules.

Then the genetic code uses these molecules and reassembles them into a completely different insect.

And out of the chrysalis comes this beautiful butterfly.

It has beautiful wings and big long legs.  It has the muscle structure that allows it to fly.  It has compound eyes that can see really well, antennae to smell with, and a big long straw to suck nectar out of flowers.

It's totally different than the ugly caterpillar it started out as.

This wonderful change reminds me of what the apostle Paul wrote in the Bible.  [Read again]

When we accept Jesus as our Savior, He changes us from an ugly caterpillar to a beautiful butterfly.

The Bible says that we have all disobeyed God; we have broken His commandments; we have sinned.

Maybe you disobeyed your parents; maybe you told a lie; maybe you took something that didn't belong to you; maybe you called somebody names or made fun of them; maybe you watched TV on Sabbath instead of coming to church…

And when we sin, this is what we look like to God: a big fat ugly caterpillar.

But here's the good news: we don't have to stay fat ugly caterpillars.

We can become beautiful butterflies that fly up to heaven.

And how do we become these new creations?  Listen again to the Bible:

"If anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation."

Where do we need to be?  In Christ.

That means that we need to love Jesus and ask Him to forgive us of our sins.

You know He died for our sins, right?  He took our punishment on the cross.

So when we believe in Him and ask Him to forgive us, He can and He does.

And when God forgives us, He sees us as a beautiful butterfly, not as an ugly caterpillar.

Which would you rather be?  Let's ask Jesus to come into our hearts and make us beautiful butterflies that will fly to heaven when Jesus comes.

title: Safe in Jesus
theme: We can never give up loving Jesus.  Only those who love Him to the end will be saved.
object: Bucket with water
body:
Isn't this a wonderful verse from the Bible?

It says that if you love Jesus and trust Him and ask Him to be your Savior, there is nothing in the universe that can separate you from Him.

When people choose to love Jesus, it makes the devil very angry.

He wants to separate us from Jesus so we'll be lost with him.

So the devil is always trying to get us to disobey God and be our own boss instead of obeying God.

But this verse tells us that God is much stronger than the devil.

God is stronger than anything in the universe.

And if we choose to trust in Jesus and be a Christian, there's nothing that can separate us from God.

If we keep loving and trusting and obeying Jesus, then when He comes back, He'll give us eternal life instead of eternal death.

So I brought this bucket today, and we're going to pretend this bucket represents Jesus.

I'm going to put this water in the bucket, and we are the water.

As long as we are in Jesus we are safe.  Jesus will save us and give us eternal life.

But what if Satan comes along and tries to tip over the bucket and pour us out?

As long as we love Jesus and trust in Him, He can keep us in the bucket even when it's upside down!!!

Now I'm going to swing this bucket with the water in it.  And when it gets to the tip it will be upside down.  And if the water comes out, I'm going to get soaked, and so are you!! If you've got an umbrella, you better put it up!!

So here we go.  Jesus is the bucket and we are the water.

And we're going to see if Jesus can hold us in the bucket even when the bucket is upside down.

Not a drop of water fell out of the bucket when it was upside down!

Do you know why the water didn't fall out?  Centrifugal force.  When I swing the bucket, centrifugal force pushes the water to the bottom of the bucket and it doesn't fall out.

When I swing the bucket, centrifugal force is greater than the force of gravity and the water stays in the bucket.

Centrifugal force is like the power of Jesus to keep us safe when we trust Him and love Him and obey Him.

Do you want Jesus to keep you in the bucket?

title: No Room for Jesus!
theme: We must make room in our hearts for Jesus.  He really wants to come in.
object: Pictures of the night Jesus was born: turned away, birth in the stable, shepherds and angels.  Jesus standing at the door knocking.
body:
This is a precious verse from the Bible.

Jesus is speaking here.  He is knocking at the door of your heart.  He wants to come in and be your friend.  He wants to save you from your sins and give you eternal life.

Here is a picture that an artist has painted to help us see what this verse is like.

Jesus is standing outside the door.  He's knocking on the door.  He's saying, "I'm Jesus.  I want to come in and be your Friend."

But it's really not the door of a house like in this picture; it is the door of your heart.

This picture of Jesus knocking on the door reminds me of the night that Jesus was born in Bethlehem.

Joseph, the earthly father of Jesus, was knocking on the door of the inn in the little town of Bethlehem.

The inn was where travelers could stay for the night.  They would have a nice bed with covers and a pillow.  There would be a place in the inn where they could buy food in the morning for breakfast.

Joseph and Mary, his wife, had traveled a long way from Nazareth, and they were very tired.

And Mary was pregnant.  The baby Jesus was in her belly and He was going to be born that very night.

This picture shows Joseph and Mary outside the inn.  The innkeeper is telling them that all the rooms are full.  There's no room for Jesus, Mary, and Joseph.

Imagine how disappointed they must have been.  Where could Mary have the baby?

The inn keeper said, "I can let you stay in the barn with the animals.  I can bring you some towels and blankets and swaddling cloths for the baby.  The hay is soft, and you can use the manger as a bed for the baby.

You know what I think?  I think that if the inn keeper knew that it was Jesus, the Son of God, being born that night, he would have slept in the barn himself and let Jesus, Mary, and Joseph have his room!

But he didn't know until the next day.

So here is a picture of the baby Jesus with Mary his mother and Joseph His earthly father in the barn where Jesus was born.

The Bible tells us that while this was going on, there were shepherds out in the fields watching their flocks at night.

An Angel appeared to them and told them the good news about the birth of Jesus.

The angel said, "There is born for you in Bethlehem tonight a Savior who is the Messiah, the Lord.

Suddenly the whole sky was filled with angels singing "Glory to God in the highest!!"

Here's another picture of what it might have been like.

The shepherds ran into Bethlehem to see this baby Jesus that the angels told them about, and they found Jesus in the barn with Mary and Joseph.

And they told everyone they could what the angels told them: that the Messiah had been born in Bethlehem.

When He grew up, Jesus was indeed the Messiah.  He became our Savior because He died on the cross to save us from our sins.

That brings us back to our first picture: Jesus standing at the door and knocking.

It would be really sad if we told Jesus, "You can't come in because there's no room in my heart for you."

Instead, Jesus would be so happy if you would say, "Come into my heart Lord Jesus, there is room in my heart for You!"

Will you make room in your heart for Jesus today?  Let's invite Him in.

title: Humility and Pride
theme: God wants us to be humble so we will trust in HIm.
object: Binoculars
body:
There are two very important words in this verse: "proud" and "humble."

The Bible says that God opposes or resists the proud but shows favor to the humble.

But it is just as true that the proud resist God and the humble receive Him.

God wants us to be humble so we can receive His grace and love.

Pride and humility are two opposites.

Pride is thinking how great you are, and humility is thinking how small you are.

I brought something in the bag this morning that I hope will help us understand the difference between pride and humility.

[Binoculars: Discuss uses.  Bird watching, moon and stars, spy on neighbors.]

Here's an interesting thing about binoculars: you can look through both ends.

When you look through the small end, everything seems large; when you look through the large end, everything looks small.

Now what does that have to do with pride and humility?

The proud person looks at himself through the small end, and he says to himself: "Look how big and important and good I am.  I'm better than all the little people around me."

And he looks at God through the large end and says to himself: "Look how small and unimportant God is.  I don't really need to depend on Him."

So the proud person ends up thinking he doesn't need God.

The humble person looks at God through the small end and says to himself: "Look how big and important and mighty and holy and loving God is."

And he looks at himself through the big end and says: "Look how little and feeble and tiny and weak and sinful I am.

So the humble person ends up knowing that he needs God to make his life a success, while the proud person doesn't think he needs God at all.

That makes a big difference.

If you think you don't need God, you're not going to trust in Jesus as your Savior and Friend.

But if you are humble, and know that you are weak and sinful and need a Savior, then you will trust Jesus to be your Savior and forgive your sins.

The proud person says: "I am great, I don't need God."

The humble person says: "God is great, I really need Him."

Which do you want to be this morning?

How many want to say: "God is great, not me.  I want Jesus to be my Savior and Friend?"

title: The Sword of the Spirit
theme: The Bible is the greatest book because it is God's word.
object: A sword
body:
Have you ever been to a library?  What do you find in a library?

In a library, you can find all kinds of books.

And if you have a library card, you can check out a book, take it home and read it, and bring it back next week.

And you can do it all for free.

The largest library in the US is the Library of Congress: It has over 34 million books!!

You could never read that many books, so we have to choose which books are worth reading.

Of the 34 million books in the Library of Congress, which is the greatest?

There is one book that, year after year, sells more copies than any other book in the world.

It is the one book that people want more than any other book.

Can you guess which book it is?  It's the Bible.

Over 100 million Bibles are sold or given away every year all over the world.

It is far-and-away the greatest book in the world!

What makes the Bible so special?  Why does everyone want to read it?

The Bible is special because it contains messages from God: It is God's word.

The things in the Bible are not just what some person thought up.

The things in the Bible are God's ideas of what He wants us to know, what He wants us to do, and how He wants us to live.

The most important thing God wants us to know is that Jesus, His Son, died for our sins, and we can be forgiven if we love Him and trust Him and tell Him we're sorry.

Now the verse that we read from the Bible this morning says that the Bible, the word of God, is alive and powerful and sharper than a two-edged sword.

Would you like to see a two-edged sword?  [Explain how soldiers fought before guns]

Now here's the question: How is the Bible like a sharp sword?

Well a sharp sword can cut things open so you can see what's inside.

Our Bible verse said the word of God is alive and powerful, and like a sharp sword, "It exposes our innermost thoughts and desires."

And when we see the selfishness and sin in our hearts and ask Jesus to forgive us, God's word has the Power to change us and give us eternal life.

God's word is alive and powerful because when we read it, we realize how much we need Jesus.

Do you want Jesus to be your Savior?  O, I want Him to be mine!

Let's thank Him for the Bible that tells us about Jesus our Savior!

title: The Helmet of Salvation
theme: Jesus took the punishment for our sins so we don't have to.
object: Hardhat
body:
This verse tells us that Jesus suffered for our sins.

Jesus was the only person that never did anything wrong.

He never disobeyed even one of God's commandments.

He was always kind and loving and good.

And before they hung Him on the cross, the soldiers beat Him with a whip until the blood poured out of His back.

God allowed Jesus to take the punishment for our sins so we could go free.

Now I have in my bag today a very unusual type of hat.  It's called a hardhat.

A hardhat is used to protect your head.  If something hits you in the head really hard, it can kill you.

Let's say you are working on building a house.  What if one of the men working on the roof accidentally dropped his hammer, and it came down and hit you on the head?  You could be dead!!

That's why everybody that works in construction has to wear a hardhat.

Now I can take this hammer and whack myself on the head.

As long as I'm wearing this hardhat, the hammer doesn't kill me.

[Strike several blows to the hardhat.]

What do you think my head would look like right now if I wasn't wearing the hardhat? (Bloody mess)

That's why this hardhat reminds me of Jesus.

He took the punishment for our sins.

The Bible says He was wounded and bruised and beaten and crucified for our sins.

He took the punishment that we deserved so that God could forgive us and save us from our sins.

Just like this hardhat took the beating from the hammer and saved my head from injury, Jesus took the beating for us.

That's how much He loves us: He took our beating and died in our place so that we can be forgiven and have eternal life with Him.

I love Him for that!  Do you love Him too?

title: One Came Back
theme: In everything, give thanks.
object: Pictures of the 10 Lepers and the one that came back (Downloaded from online PPT by Henry Martin Sermons4Kids).
body:
Who knows what special holiday is coming up on Thursday of next week?  (Thanksgiving)

Our whole country celebrates Thanksgiving on the fourth Thursday of November.  Why do we do that?

Thanksgiving was made a national holiday by President Abraham Lincoln back in 1863, during the civil war.

President Lincoln said that this day should be a day of "Thanksgiving and Praise to our beneficent Father who dwells in the Heavens."

It was to be a day for being thankful to God and for praising Him.

Now it is important to be thankful to each other, too.  If somebody does something nice for your, I hope you always say "Thank you."  And if someone says "Thank you," to you, what should you say back to them? (You're welcome!)

In the Bible, Luke records something that happened to Jesus.

On his way to Jerusalem, Jesus traveled along the border between Samaria and Galilee.

As he was going into a village, he met ten men who had leprosy.   [Show picture of 10.  Explain leprosy]

Because leprosy was such a bad disease, they weren't allowed to come up to talk to Jesus, so they stood at a distance and called out in a loud voice, "Jesus, Master, have pity on us!"

When Jesus saw them, he said, "Go, show yourselves to the priests."

And as they went, they were healed.  [Show picture of healed lepers]

One of them, when he saw he was healed, came back, praising God in a loud voice.

He threw himself at Jesus' feet and thanked him.  [Show picture of one kneeling]

Jesus said, were not all ten healed?  Where are the other nine?

Isn't that sad?  Only one came back to thank Jesus for the greatest miracle they had ever received.

I hope you'll be like the one leper who came back to thank Jesus for healing him.

The Bible verse we read this morning said, "In everything give thanks" for this is what Jesus wants you to do.

Think about this for a moment: What if you woke up in the morning, and the only things you had were the things you thanked God for the day before?

What if yesterday you forgot to say the blessing and thank God for your food?  Then no food for your today!

What if yesterday you forgot to thank God for your family and friends?  Then nobody for you to talk to today!

What if yesterday you forgot to thank God for your nice warm house?  Where would you be living and sleeping today?

What if yesterday you forgot to thank God for the clothes you wear, for the car that takes you to school, for your teachers, for your toys, for your TV, for life, and health, and strength?  You'd have nothing.

We can be thankful that God loves us so much He doesn't take away what we don't thank Him for.

But I hope you'll be like the one leper that came back and thanked Jesus.

Will you say a prayer this Thanksgiving and thank God for everything you have?  Even your toothbrush?

Are you thankful for your toothbrush?  Then let's thank Him for that too.

Everyday should be Thanksgiving day for Christians.  Let's thank God right now.

title: Mary had a Little Lamb (Adapted from Sermons4Kids.com)
theme: Mary's baby boy was the Lamb of God who would die to take away the sin of the world.
object: Small stuffed lamb.
body:
This is what the angel told Mary and Joseph: when the baby is born, name Him Jesus because He will save His people from their sins.

So that's the name Mary and Joseph gave to the little baby that Mary had.

Thirty years later, when Jesus was all grown up, John the Baptist pointed to Jesus and told everybody, "Look, the Lamb of God that takes away the sin of the world."

Now you may wonder why John would call Jesus the Lamb of God.  How is Jesus like this little lamb? [show lamb]

Before Jesus was born, when someone sinned, they brought a lamb to the temple as a sacrifice to show they were sorry for their sins.

They confessed their sin over the lamb and the lamb was killed for their sin.

In the same way, Jesus was the true Lamb of God who died for our sins on the cross.

Have you ever heard the poem, Mary had a Little Lamb?

It is one of the best-known children's poems.  Here's how it starts:

Mary had a little lamb, [show lamb]
Its fleece was white as snow.
And everywhere that Mary went
The lamb was sure to go.

How many are looking forward to Christmas?

Why do we celebrate Christmas?  (It's a day to remember that Jesus was born to become our Savior)

What was Jesus' mother's name?  (Mary)

And if Jesus is the Lamb of God, when He was born He was a "little Lamb."

So I want to read you a new poem called "Mary had a Little Lamb" but it's about Mary the mother of Jesus and Jesus is the Little Lamb.

Mary had a little Lamb, 
He was born on Christmas day.
She laid him in a manger bed
To Sleep upon the hay.

Angels filled the night-time sky
And they began to sing.
Shepherds heard them all proclaim
The birthday of a King.

Wise men saw a blazing star
Up in the sky that night.
They followed it until they found
The King of love and light.

Mary had a little Lamb,
But He wasn't hers, you know,
He was the very Son of God,
The One who loves us so.

The Father of this little Lamb
Loved the world so much
That He sent his only Son to earth
So we could feel His touch.

He came to give us joy and peace
And take away our sin.
So when He knocks on your heart's door,
Be sure to let Him in.

The true meaning of Christmas is not the lights or the tree or the cookies or Santa or presents.

The true meaning is that God so loved the world that He gave His only Son (the Lamb of God, Jesus) to die for our sin… so when He knocks at your heart's door, be sure to let Him in.

How many would like to invite Jesus into your heart today?

title: Herod the Grinch (Adapted from Sermons4kids.com)
theme: Don't be a grinch, let Jesus give you and new heart.
object: Picture of the book or movie How the Grinch Stole Christmas
body:
How many have ever read the book or seen the movie of "How the Grinch Stole Christmas?"

Here's a picture of the Grinch.  [Show picture.]

And what's he doing?  He's stealing a Christmas tree from someone in Who-ville.

Now everyone in Who-ville loved Christmas, because it was such a joyful time.

But the Grinch hated Christmas, so he made up a plan to ruin the joy of Christmas in Who-ville.

His plan was to dress up like Santa Claus and go into Who-ville at night and steal all of their Christmas presents, all of their Christmas decorations, and even the food for their Christmas dinner.

Do you know why the Grinch hated Christmas so much?

Well, according to the story, it was because his heart was too small!  (2 sizes too small!)

He was so selfish that he hated to see anyone else who was happy and enjoying themselves.

But, as you know, the Grinch's plan did not work.

Why? Because the people in Who-ville knew that the real joy of Christmas does not come from the presents, decorations, and food -- it comes from a heart filled with love.

The story of "How the Grinch Stole Christmas" is not a true story, but this morning I want to tell you a true story of another "Grinch" who tried to steal Christmas.

In fact, he tried to steal the very first Christmas.

This "Grinch" was a king by the name of Herod the Great.  We'll call him "Herod the Grinch."

After Jesus was born, wise men from the east came to Jerusalem and asked where they could find the child who had been born to be the king of the Jews. They said, "We have seen his star in the sky and want to worship him."  When Herod the Grinch heard about this, he told the wise men that when they found the child, they should come back and tell him where the child was so that he could worship him too.   That was a lie.

The truth is, Herod the Grinch didn't really want to worship Jesus, he wanted to kill him.

Why? Because Herod the Grinch's heart was too small!

Herod the Grinch was so selfish that he was afraid Jesus would become more popular than he was and take over his kingdom.

Well, Herod's plan to kill Jesus didn't work. After the wise men found Jesus and gave him gifts, an angel told them about Herod's plan and they went back home without telling Herod where they had found Jesus. An angel also appeared to Joseph and warned him of Herod's plan and told him to take Mary and the baby Jesus to Egypt where they would be safe.

You know what's sad about this story?

Herod the Grinch was trying to kill the very one who could give him a bigger heart.

Herod needed a new heart, and Jesus is in the business of giving people new hearts.

He can take our tiny, little, selfish, stone-cold heart and make it a big soft warm heart that loves God and loves other people.

Herod the Grinch needed Jesus to give him a bigger heart.

What about us?  We don't want to be grinches do we?

Would you like Jesus to give you a bigger heart?

Let's ask Jesus to give us a bigger heart that will love God most of all and love others as ourselves.

title: The Eyes of Faith
theme: Trust is like allowing someone to lead us when we are blind.
object: Blindfold
body:
God morning kids!  It is so good to see you in church today!

My how nice you all look!

And as I look at you, I can see your eyes looking back at me.  Can you see me this morning?

I'm so thankful I can see, aren't you?

What a wonderful thing God created when He created eyes that can see!

But you know kids, there are some people who are blind.  They're eyes don't work.

And when they open their eyes, they can's see any better than when their eyes are closed.

Did you ever wonder what it would be like to be blind?

One way we can see what it would be like is to put on a blindfold.

So I need a volunteer this morning who is willing to be blindfolded.

And that volunteer has to be willing to trust me to lead them in the right direction when they can't see.

[Blindfold volunteer.  Ask if they trust me to lead them around safely in the church.]

Now before I lead [volunteer] around, I want to tell you what happened to me when I was a kid.

When I was a kid, maybe 5 years old, I played a game similar to this with my younger sister Jeanie.

We were outside, and I had the Indian blanket over my head so I couldn't see where I was going, and Jeanie was leading me around.

It was fun until she led me toward the house.  Along the side of the house was the sidewalk, and the edge of the sidewalk was higher than the ground I was walking on.  So I tripped on the sidewalk, fell forward and cracked my head full force on the foundation of the house, which was cement.  That sent me to bed with a concussion.  I never trusted my sister after that to lead me around under the Indian blanket.

I hope [volunteer] has a better experience that I did!

Do you still trust me to lead you around safely?

[Lead volunteer around the church safely.]

Did [volunteer] trust me?  Did I lead [him/her] safely around the church?

Even though [volunteer] couldn't see where [he/she] was going, she trusted me and I led [him/her] safely.

The Bile says that w/o faith or w/o trust it is impossible to please God.

God wants us to trust Him just like [volunteer] trusted me.

Let's read again what the Bible says:

Proverbs 3:5-6 (NKJV) Trust in the Lord with all your heart, And lean not on your own understanding; In all your ways acknowledge Him, And He shall direct your paths.

This is God's promise: If you trust Him with all your heart, He will lead you safely to His heavenly kingdom.

How many want to trust God with all their heart this morning?

title: Doubting Thomas
theme: Even though we have not seen Jesus, we can believe He is alive because the Bible is true.
object: Large wine glass, dry paper clips, small square of dry paper towel (each edge longer than the paper clip), water to fill glass to about 1 in. from the top.
body:
This is the passage in the Bible from which we get the expression "Doubting Thomas."

Someone might say, "O that person is a doubting Thomas.  He won't believe it unless he sees it."

You remember that Jesus died for our sins on the cross on Friday, and God brought Him back to life on Sunday morning.

On that same Sunday evening, the disciples were gathered together in a room in a house with the doors shut and all of a sudden Jesus just appeared in the middle of the room.

He showed His disciples the nail prints in His hands and the hole where the spear went into His side and they all believed that Jesus had risen from the dead.

But one of the disciples named Thomas wasn't there.

When the other disciples told Thomas that they had seen Jesus alive, Thomas said, ""Unless I see the nail marks in his hands and put my finger where the nails were, and put my hand into his side, I will not believe."

Thomas wouldn't believe the word of the other disciples until he saw it for himself.

Are you like that sometimes?  I know I am.

If someone tells me something that seems impossible, I might just say, "I won't believe that until I see it."

Well I brought something in my bag this morning that seems impossible to believe.

I'm going to ask you to believe that a paper clip can float on water.  [Set up experiment.]

Now that seems impossible, doesn't it?  A paper clip is made out of metal, and metal is denser than water.  So the paper clip should always sink in water, right?

So let's do the experiment and see what happens.

[Have a volunteer carefully drop a paperclip into the glass of water; it sinks immediately.  Repeat several times.]

So, do paperclips float on water or do they sink?  (They sink.)

So, if I told you a paperclip could float on water would you believe me?

Most people would say "No! I don't believe a paper clip can float.  I won't believe that until I see it!"

Most of us would be "Doubting Thomases."

So give me another paperclip and I will try to make this one float.

[Put paperclip on paper towel and gently place paper tower on water surface.  The paper towel sinks and the paper clip floats.]

NOW how many believe that a paperclip can float on water?  (We all believe it because we have seen it.)

Let's go back to the story of doubting Thomas.

One whole week went by and Thomas doubted the whole week long.

The next Sunday evening the disciples were together again and Thomas was with them this time.

Jesus appeared again in the middle of the room and He told Thomas: "Look at my hands, look at my side; touch the nail prints, stop doubting and believe!

Thomas saw and believed.  He said, "My Lord and my God."

Then Jesus said, "Blessed are those who have not seen and yet believe."

We haven't seen Jesus, we haven't touched the nail prints in His hands, but we can believe that He is alive.

Why? Because the Bible is God's word, and the Bible tells us that Jesus is alive.

How many believe that Jesus is alive even though we haven't seen Him yet?

Let's believe so that when He comes again He will save us in His Kingdom!

title: The Mustard Seed
theme: The kingdom of Jesus will grow to fill the whole earth
object: Display card with different size seeds glued next to label of kind of seed it is: green bean, zucchini, cucumber, kale, arugula, lettuce.  Measuring tape to illustrate height of mustard plant.
body:
How many of you kids have ever planted a seed to grow vegetables or flowers?

Seeds are really amazing.

Seeds can remain just seeds for a very long time.

But if you plant them in the ground and give them water and sunlight, they begin to grow into a plant.

And the plant grows and grows and eventually it produces, fruits or vegetables or nuts or grains and more seeds like the one that you planted.

It is just amazing that God has created in each little seed the information it needs to produce something good for us to eat and give us more seeds to plant next year.

So I wanted to show you today some of the seeds that I planted in my garden this year.

[Show seeds.  Describe what each produces.  Note how big the seeds are.  Tell how big the plants are that they produce.]

Now Jesus said that the kingdom of God was like a mustard seed.

A mustard seed is only about as big as this little lettuce seed at the bottom.

But Jesus said that this little seed would grow into a big tree where birds could build their nests.

Jesus said that the kingdom of God was like this mustard seed.

It starts out really small but it grows really big.

If you look at Jesus when He started His ministry, it was only one person: Jesus.

Then He added the 12 disciples and the women who followed Him.

After Jesus rose from the dead, there were 120 disciples praying in the upper room.

On the day of Pentecost when the Holy Spirit was poured out, 3,000 people were added to the kingdom.

A short while later there were 5,000 men and their families that were His disciples.

And the kingdom kept growing over the years until today there are over 2.4 billion people who claim to be Christians.

Now there is also another way that the kingdom of God grows.

It grows in our hearts.

We may start out not knowing very much about Jesus.

Maybe we only know: "Jesus loves me this I know, For the Bible tells me so."

It may be a small beginning, but our faith in Jesus grows as we pray and learn what the Bible says and learn to love Jesus more.

The kingdom of God grows in our heart until we are like a big tree that stands tall for Jesus and can never be blown down by the devil.

And when Jesus comes back, all those standing tall for Him will get to be part of His kingdom, where Jesus is our King and we'll live for Him forever.

Does that sound good to you?  Are you interested?

Let's ask Jesus to help us grow from little mustard seeds to tall trees for His kingdom.

title: The Shield of Faith
theme: Jesus can protect us from all the temptations Satan uses to try to destroy us.
object: Container of Bt (Bacillus thuringiensis) concentrate used to kill caterpillar worms, sprayer, picture of Bt bacteria.  Cauliflower leaf eaten by cabbage worms.
body:
Who is the evil one?  (The devil.  We also call him Satan.)

The devil is out to destroy us.

He wants us to turn out back on Jesus and follow him instead.

Sao he is always tempting us to sin and love other things more than we love Jesus.

It's like we're in a battle or a war with the devil.

The devil is like shooting flaming arrows at us to set us on fire and destroy us.

And Paul is telling Christians that they need to put on the whole armor of God to be protected from the devil.

Especially in the verse we read today, we are to take the shield of faith in order to stop all the flaming arrows he shoots at us.

Now how many remember a couple of weeks ago we talked about the cabbage butterfly and what it has been doing to my garden.  Does anyone remember?

The butterfly laid eggs on all my broccoli plants, and these little caterpillar worms came out of the eggs and started to eat holes in all the broccoli leaves.

Then they got into the heads of broccoli, the part that you eat, and all my broccoli was full of worms!

I only got one head of broccoli out of 12 broccoli plants.  Now I've had to throw them all on the mulch pile.

The same thing happened to my Cauliflower!  Look at this leaf!  [Show cauliflower leaf full of holes.]

Then I looked closely at the kale.  Guess what: holes in the leaves.

The caterpillars were eating my kale. That's enough, I said.

So I bought something that would protect my garden from cabbage caterpillars.

Here it is!  [Show container of Monterey Bt.]

This is a special kind of pesticide that kills caterpillars.

It's not a chemical, it is a particular kind of bacteria found in the soil.  It's called Bacillus thuringiensis.

This s like biological warfare!

These bacteria are so small you can't see them without a microscope, but here's a picture of Bacillus thuringiensis magnified many times.  [Show picture.]

This bacterium is harmless to human beings and other animals, but when the caterpillars eat one of these, it releases proteins that make the caterpillars sick.

And when they get sick, they stop eating and die.

So I mix this Bt with water, put it in a sprayer, and spray all the leaves of the kale and the cauliflower.

And it acts like a shield to kill all the caterpillar worms that try to eat the leaves.

This reminds me of what the Bible says about taking the shield of faith.

Satan is shooting flaming arrows at us to try to destroy us.

But faith in Jesus is our shield to protect us from his temptations.

When Satan tempts us to follow him, we can hide behind Jesus our shield.

If Bt can defeat the caterpillars how much more can Jesus defeat the devil.

How many want to make Jesus their shield from the devils flaming arrows?

title: The Lost Sheep
theme: All heaven rejoices when a sinner repents and is brought back to the fold by Jesus.
object: Various small stuffed animals.  One, a little green monkey with purple hair, hidden under the communion table.
body:
This morning I brought with me a collection of animals that I really like.

Each one is special and unique.  Would you like to see them?

[Show each stuffed animal and tell a little bit about it.  Look in the empty bag with shock that the monkey is missing but the pencil he hangs on is still there.]

O no!  My little monkey is missing!! Here's the green pencil he usually hangs on.  The pencil is here but my monkey is gone!  Maybe he crawled out of the bag.

Will you help me look for my monkey?

He's a green monkey with purple hair and a big long tail.

My collection of animals will never be the same with out Monk, the monkey.  Who can help me find him?

[Be happy and joyful when Monk is found.]

Do you know, my lost monkey reminds me of a story that Jesus told.

There were some religious leaders that were upset with Jesus because he hung out with bad people who were sinners.

Jesus would even share a meal with them.

So Jesus told them a story about a shepherd who had 100 sheep, but one night when he counted them up, he counted only 99 sheep.

One sheep was lost!!

So the shepherd left the 99 in the sheep pen and went looking for the one lost sheep until he found it.

And when he found it, he put it on his shoulders and carried it back to the sheep pen.

He was really happy he found the lost sheep and he told his shepherd friends and neighbors, and they were all happy too.

And the religious leaders were probably thinking, "Yea, I'd be happy too if I found my sheep that was lost.

And then Jesus hit them with the punch line.  He said,

"There is more rejoicing in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous persons who do not need to repent."

That's why Jesus hung out with sinners: because they were the lost sheep that needed to repent.

The Bible teaches us that we are like sheep and sometimes stray away from God.

That makes God very sad, but he never gives up on us.

He keeps searching for us and calling to us.

He wants to draw us back into his arms.

He even sent Jesus, his Son, to save those who are lost.

Aren't you glad that God doesn't give up on his lost sheep?

I am, because "I once was lost, but now I'm found!"

Let's thank Jesus that He looks for us when we are lost, forgives our sins, and brings us back home.

title: Living Water
theme: Jesus can give us living water that quenches our thirst for God.
object: Bottled water and a plastic cup for each child.
body:
Who knows what special day is coming up tomorrow on December 31?

It's New Year's Eve, and at midnight tomorrow night, the year 2023 will end and the year 2024 will begin.

Many pwople want to make 2024 a better year than 2023, so they make New Year's resolutions.

A resolution is a promise that people make to themselves; it's a promise to do something better in the new year.

They might say, "In 2024, I'm going to stop smoking." Well that's a good resolution isn't it?  If you stop smoking, you're going to be a lot healthier and live longer."

Or they might say, "In 2024, I'm going to lose 20 pounds." Well, that's a good resolution too.  Being overweight is bad for your health too.

Or they might say, "In 2024 I'm going to go to the Gym 3 days a week and get in shape."  Well, that's a good resolution too.  Exercise is good for you and makes you healthy and strong.

If you want to have good health in the new year, I highly recommend the NEW START acronym.

Each letter in NEW START reminds us of the eight principles for good health.

N stands for Nutrition: You want to eat plenty of fruits and vegetables, grains and nuts, not so much meat.

E stands for exercise: Get plenty of exercise instead of watching TV.

W stands for water: Drink plenty of pure clean water especially when you exercise.

S stands for Sunshine: Sunshine helps your body produce vitamin D that keeps you well.

T stands for temperance: That means don't put any drugs in your body, and don't OD on sweets.

A stands for air: Get outside and get plenty of fresh air.

R stands for rest: Get at least 8 hours of sleep at night and rest from work on the Sabbath.

And T stands for trust in God.  People who trust in God live healthier and longer lives.

This morning I want to focus on just one of these health principles: water, drinking clean, pure, water.

So I'm going to give each one of you a little cup with some water in it, and every time you hear me say the word "water" I want you to take a little sip of water.

Jesus had something important to say about water (pause).

One day Jesus was walking through a town in Samaria.

He was hot and tired so he sat down beside a well to rest.

A woman came to the well to get some water (pause), and Jesus asked her if she would give him a drink.

The woman was surprised that Jesus spoke to her because Jews would not usually speak to Samaritans.

Jesus told her that He could give her living water! (pause)

She didn't understand what He was talking about so she said, "You don't even have anything to lower into the well to get the water, (pause) how can you give me living water?" (pause)

Then Jesus said, "Whoever drinks from the water (pause) from this well will be thirsty again, but whoever drinks the water (pause) I give him will never thirst again."

Wow! Water (pause) that would satisfy your thirst so you would never be thirsty again!

But, Jesus wasn't talking about our thirst for water (pause) from a well.

He was talking about our thirst for God.

The Bible teaches us that, when we understand the goodness of God, we have a thirst in our heart for Him.

And that is a thirst that only Jesus can satisfy.

When we trust in Jesus, He becomes the living water (pause) that always satisfies our thirst for God.

How many want to trust in Jesus and satisfy their thirst for God?

title: Triumphal Entry
theme: Jesus must fulfill OT prophecy concerning the Messiah
object: Geode piece with amethyst crystals on one side.
body:
This is a prophecy that God gave the prophet Zechariah humdreds of years before Jesus was born.

But it is a prophecy that Jesus fulfilled just six days before He was crucified on Good Friday.

Do you know what a prophecy is?

It is when God tells us what He is going to do long before He does it.

And then when it happens just like He said, we can believe that God's word is true and that He can do what He says.

There are hundreds of prophecies in the Bible and when we see them coming true, we can believe that the Bible is God's word.  Does that make sense?

This prophecy in Zechariah tells how Jesus, the Messiah, would come into Jerusalem before He was crucified.

He would come into Jerusalem riding on a young donkey that had never been ridden before.

And the people around Him would be rejoicing greatly and shouting aloud the praises of their King.

This is exactly what happened according to the gospel accounts of Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John.

It is what Christians have called "The Triumphal Entry."

The disciples borrowed a young donkey that Jesus rode, and all the people waved palm branches and laid their coats in the path for the donkey to walk on.

And John tells what they were shouting:  They were shouting "Hosanna! 'Blessed is He who comes in the name of the LORD!' The King of Israel!

And you know why they were shouting and praising Jesus as the King? Because that's what the prophecy of Zechariah said: "Rejoice greatly, O daughter of Zion! Shout aloud, O daughter of Jerusalem."

Now there were some Pharisees in the crowd that didn't like Jesus and didn't think He was the king that God promised.

So they told Jesus to make the people be quiet and stop praising Him.

And Jesus had an interesting answer for them.

It's recorded in:

Luke 19:40 ESV - 40 Jesus said, "I tell you, if these were silent, the very stones would cry out."

So Jesus is saying "if these people didn't praise me, the stones on the ground would have to!"

Now He didn't mean that the stones would actually speak.

It was his way of saying it is more likely that stones would speak than for one of God's prophecies to fail.

In other words, the people had to shout and praise him as their king because that's what the prophecy of Zechariah said.

And God's prophecies are never wrong.

When God says it, we can believe it, and know that He will make it happen.

Now I did bring with me today one of my favorite stones.

It can't talk, but in a way, it can silently praise God who created it.

Would you like to see it?

If you look at the outside of the stone, it doesn't look very beautiful.

But if you flip it over this is the inside of a geode that is covered with beautiful amithist crystals.

Isn't it beautiful?

So even though this stone can't speak, it's beauty is a testimony to the glory fo God who made it.

So let me ask you this question: If you were there when Jesus rode by on a donkey, would you be silent like a stone or shout: "Jesus is Lord and our King forever!!"

title: Rooted in Jesus
object: Impatience potted flower that can be removed to show roots, cut flower.
body:
I's like to show you two flowers today.

One is a cut flower from this vase of flowers on the communion table.  [Pick a nice flower from the vase.]

Isn't this a beautiful flower?

The other flower I want to show you is in this potted flower that I bought at the nursery.

Let me pull it out of the container and show you what makes this flower grow.

This potted flower has all kinds of roots that suck up the water and nutrients from the soil.

Which of these two flowers do you think will last the longest?

Yes! The potted plant has roots and is living and growing.

The cut flower has no roots and is dying.

A few more days and the cut flower will be withered and dead and will have to be thrown in the garbage.

But when I plant this potted flower in the ground or in a flower pot, it will grow and bloom all summer long and produce lots of beautiful flowers.

The roots make all the difference.

To be living and growing and producing more blooms, flowers need to have healthy roots that suck up nutrients and water from the soil.

The Bible verse we read this morning says that, if we want to be growing Christians, we need to have good roots too.

The apostle Paul wrote to his Christian friends that they needed to keep following Jesus and let their roots grow down into Him.

Now people don't have actual roots like a flower.

But what he means is that we need to stay connected to Jesus in order to grow as Christians.

How do we let our roots grow down into Jesus?

First, we need to learn about Jesus and how He wants us to live.

We can do that by reading the Bible and Bible stories and coming to church and SS, singing songs about Jesus, going to a Christian school where they have worship, going to camp meeting and Camp Winnekeag.

We want to learn from the Bible and Bible teachers how Jesus wants us to live and how to keep His commandments.

The more we know about Jesus the more we will love Him and want to obey Him.

The second thing we can do to let our roots grow down into Jesus is to think of Him often, to pray every day, to ask Him to help us in difficult times, to ask Him to forgive us when we mess up, and do our best to be His friend.

Friends stay connected; they talk to each other; they hang out together.

Do you want to have a friend that can save you in His kingdom and give you eternal life?

Only Jesus can do that.

So which flower would you like to be?

Don't cut yourself off from Jesus!

Let your roots grow down into Him.

Let's ask Jesus to come into our hearts and be our Savior and Friend.

title: Jesus Wept
theme: Death may be sad, but the resurrection is glad.
object: Box of Kleenex; Bandana handkerchief
body:
How many of you kids have memorized Bible verses for school?

Who can tell me what the shortest verse in the Bible is?  (Jesus wept.)

It's the shortest verse in the whole Bible: just two words.  Jesus wept.

That's the easiest verse to memorize.  It is found in John 11:35. how many have memorized it?

Something made Jesus so sad that He cried:  tears were coming from His eyes and running down His cheeks.

How do you think He dried His tears?  Do you think He had a box of Kleenex like this [show box] or maybe He had a bandana in His pocket like this [show bandana].

The Bible doesn't say, but it does tell us that Jesus wept.  He cried.

Now what was it that made Jesus cry?

Well, if you read the story in John 11, Jesus cried because He had a very good friend that died.

His friend's name was Lazarus, and Lazarus got sick and died.

Now when people we love die, it's a very sad thing.  We know we're not going to see them again in this life, and we know we're going to miss them, so we are sad, and sometimes we cry.

Is it ok to cry when your friend dies?  Yea!! If it's ok for Jesus to cry, it's ok for us too, right?

So some of us cried this past week because one of our very dear friend died.

Many of you know her as teacher Ruth.  She taught Sabbath school for the kids for many years.

She was such a kind Christian woman.  Everybody that knew her loved sister Ruth.

Now you might ask, what does the future hold for teacher Ruth; is she dead and gone forever.

Well, the Bible tells us what happens when a person dies.

When Jesus told His disciples that Lazarus was dead, here's what He said.

Jesus told His disciples: "Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep, but I go to awaken him." 12 The disciples said to him, "Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will recover." 13 Now Jesus had spoken of his death, but they thought that he meant taking rest in sleep. 14 Then Jesus told them plainly, "Lazarus is dead,"

So here Jesus compares death to falling asleep.

When we die, we have no knowledge of the passage of time.

Death is like falling asleep at night, and you don't remember anything until you wake up the next morning.

Jesus actually went to Lazarus' tomb and raised Him back to life again.

And it was just like Lazarus had taken a nap for four days, he didn't remember anything, but he was glad to be alive again.

So teacher Ruth is now like Lazarus was: It's like she is sleeping and waiting for Jesus to come.

And here's the good news: When Jesus comes back, He is going to bring back to life all those who love Him and have trusted in Him to forgive their sins.

So that's what's going to happen to teacher Ruth.

When Jesus comes back, she will come back to life in a brand-new body, and the angels will carry her up to meet Jesus in the air.

And she's going to be so glad because she loves Jesus so much and she's going to get to spend eternity with Him.

So we don't have to be sad.  God has a wonderful future planned for teacher Ruth in the kingdom of God.

Now here's more good news: If we love Jesus and trust in Him for the forgiveness of our sins, Jesus will take us to His glorious kingdom too, and we can spend eternity with Ruth and Jesus all those that love Him.

Are you interested?  Would you like to live in God's kingdom where Jesus is King?

title: Lazarus Slept
theme: Death is lake a deep sleep till Jesus comes.
body:
Do you remember last week in the children's sermon we talked about the shortest verse in the Bible?

Who remembers what that verse says? (Jesus wept.)

Why did Jesus weep?  Who remembers why?

It was because His very good friend Lazarus had died.

And when people we love die, it makes us sad.

We talked about Teacher Ruth that died about a week ago, and we were sad because everybody loved teacher Ruth.

But you know what Jesus did for His friend, Lazarus?

He brought him back to life again.  He resurrected him.

John chapter 11 describes some of what happened.

Jesus and a whole procession of people went to the cave where Lazarus was buried.

He was buried in a cave with a stone rolled over the entrance.

And Jesus told some of the people to roll away the stone.

And Martha, who was the sister of Lazarus said, Lord, it's going to smell really bad in there because he has been dead 4 days and his body is starting to decay.

But Jesus told her, ""Did I not tell you that if you believed you would see the glory of God?"

So they rolled away the stone, and Jesus prayed a short prayer to His Father in heaven.

And then He shouted, "Lazarus, come out."

And here is what the apostle John says happened:

John 11:44 ESV The man who had died [Lazarus] came out, his hands and feet bound with linen strips, and his face wrapped with a cloth. Jesus said to them, "Unbind him, and let him go."

So the way they buried people in Jesus' time was to wrap them up in strips of cloth like an Egyptian mummy.

Here's a picture of what it may have looked like.

So maybe he had to hop out of the cave.

I can imagine that everybody was so glad to see him that they were shouting for joy and thanking Jesus.

And Jesus must have given him a big hug to welcome him back to life.

What did Lazarus remember from the four days he was dead?

He remembered absolutely nothing; it was like he had taken a long nap until Jesus woke him up.

And that is what teacher Ruth is doing right now.

She's dead, and it is like she is taking a long nap, waiting for Jesus to come and wake her up.

And when Jesus comes, He is going to give here a new body, a strong body, a glorious body, a young body that will never get old and never die again.

Now Lazarus didn't get that kind of a body; his body was just a regular body like ours, so he got old and died again.

But the Bible says that when Jesus comes again and resurrects those who love Him, He gives us immortal bodies that will never die or get sick or get old.

Would you like to have that kind of body?  I sure would.

Let's ask Jesus to come into our hearts and be our Savior so we can have that kind of body when He comes again.

title: Choose Carefully
theme: Beware: Satan sometimes makes evil choices look good.
object: A giant mushroom from my mulch pile.  Macrolepiota procera. Parasol mushroom. Pictures from website for a true Parasol mushroom.
body:
Sometimes it is not always easy to make the right decisions in life.

The Bible says that Satan, the devil, sometimes appears as an angel of light.

The devil has a way of making bad things seem not so bad.

He might use a friend to get you to listen to the wrong kind of music.

In commercials on TV it looks like people drinking beer are always having fun.

But the truth is that alcohol causes a lot of diseases, a lot of accidents in which people get killed, and a lot of fights between husbands and wives.

Satan tries to make sin look good, but the Bible says that sin, when it is finished, brings death.

Do you know what I found in my back yard yesterday? I brought it in my bag this morning.

This is a giant mushroom, and it looks like it could be good to eat.

But listen, there are also poisonous mushrooms that look like they're good to eat.

There have been lots of cases where people find mushrooms in their yard or in the woods.

They think they are good to eat, so they cook them and eat them.

But then they get sick, and some have even died.

That's why we always buy our mushrooms at the store, because we know they're good to eat.

So how can we tell if this mushroom is good to eat or not?  Should we just try it and see if we get sick or die?

No! We have to study the mushroom and compare it to the kinds of mushrooms that are good to eat.

So here I have some pictures that I printed from a website that tells all about the macrolepiota procera mushroom.  This website says that these mushrooms are very good to eat.

So let's compare the pictures of this good mushroom to the mushroom I found.

Before we eat a mushroom that looks good, we always should check to see if it's really good or if it's poisonous.

The same is true of alcohol, beer or wine.

It may look good in the commercials on TV, but the Bible says it is really a poison.

Satan is always trying to make sin look good.

But don't be fooled.

Even if your friends want you to disobey God's commandments, don't do it.

Disobeying God is like eating a poisonous mushroom.  In the end, sin kills.

That's why we need Jesus.

Only Jesus can forgive our sins and give us eternal life instead of eternal death.

Do you want to make good choices in life?  Let's start by choosing Jesus.

title: Actions Speak Louder than Words
theme: Actions are better than words in expressing our love.
object: Homemade Mothers' Day card.
body:
These are Jesus' words to His disciples.

He told them that if they really loved Him they would keep His commandments and do what He says.

Have you ever heard the expression: "Actions speak louder than words?"

I think that's what Jesus is saying in this verse.

It's easy to say, "I love you, Jesus." But it's a lot harder to show we love Jesus by keeping His commandments and doing what He says.
