Who can tell me what special day we celebrate tomorrow? (Mothers' Day)

What do you think Mothers' Day is all about?

It's a day when we can show how much we appreciate our mothers or grandmothers and all that they do for us.

How many are planning to make a card for their mom?

That's a really great way to tell your mom how much you love her.

You could just say, "I love you, Mom," and I am sure she would appreciate that.

But if you take the time to make a card, that's even better.  Why?  Because actions speak louder than words.

Your actions in taking the time and making the effort to make a card says "I love you" better than just saying the words.

Let me show you a beautiful card that Bella made for Grama.  [Show card.]

Now besides making a card, what are some of the other ways you can show your mom that you love her?

Remember that actions speak louder than words.  What can you do to show your love?

Do you think you're old enough to help out around the house?  Would mom like that?

How many have toys they play with?  Do you always pick up your toys and put them away when you're done playing?  And if you don't put them away, who has to?  Mom, right?

If we just throw our clothes on the floor when we take them off, who has to pick them up?

There are all kinds of tings we can do to show our moms that we love them: We can clean up our toys and hang up our clothes and help with some of the housework.

Maybe you could run the vacuum or sweep the floor or do the dusting or help clean off the table after a meal or put your trash in the garbage can.

And just like Jesus said, one of the best ways to show your love for your mom is to obey her.

When she says, "Supper's ready, come to the table."  Do it!  Don't just sit there watching TV and make her call you 3 or 4 times.

When she says, "Stop fighting."  Stop!  Get along with your sisters and brothers.

So obeying your mom and dad and grandparents is a wonderful way to show that you love them.

And that takes us back to what Jesus said to His disciples: "If you love me you will keep my commandments."

Actions speak louder than words.

It's easy to say, "I love you, Jesus."

But Jesus wants more than just words.  He wants us to show that we really love Him by keeping His commandments.

How many want to show Jesus that they really love Him by doing what He says?

Let's ask Him to help us obey God and obey our moms and dads too.

title: The Magic Gossip Box
theme: God wants our words to build others up, not tear them down.
object: Cardboard box with a hand hole in each end labeled "Magic Gossip Box." Large and small size of various objects: nail, measuring tape, wrench, screw driver.  Have fun with this.
body:
Who here is able to talk?

Everyone is able to talk, right?

So this verse from the Bible says don't let any unwholesome or bad talk come out of your mouths.

That means don't use dirty words, don't take God's name in vain, don't say OMG, don't use bad language.

And this verse goes on to say that our words should build others up, not tear them down.

God wants our words to help others not to hurt them.

How many of you kids have heard the word "gossip?"

Does anyone know what gossip is?

Gossip is when you talk about other people and tell things that may not be true.

And even if they are true, the story is intended to hurt them and make them look bad.

Then that person tells the story to someone else and the story turns into a big lie.

Let me give you an example: Let's say that John and Mary are a boy and girl in your school.

And one day you see them walking together at recess.

So you tell one of your friends, "Hey, guess what.  I saw John and Mary walking together at recess and I think they like each other."

Then that friend tells another friend, "Hey guess what.  John and Mary like each other and they were holding hands at recess."

Then that friend tells another friend, "Hey guess what.  John and Mary love each other and they were kissing in the woods at recess."

So gossip can start with a little innocent story and grow into a big lie that hurts the people we gossip about.

So I brought with me the magic gossip box today that shows what happens when we tell stories about other people.

[Put small object in one end of box.  "This is John and Mary talking at recess."  Do some loud whispering and bla, bla, bla, and gossip, gossip, gossip.  Pull the similar large object out the other end of the box.  "This is John and Mary kissing in the woods."   Emphasize for each object that gossip makes the lie bigger the more it gets told.]

Do you think God wants us to gossip about other people and tell stories to make them look bad?

No, God wants our words to be kind and helpful to others.

That's why we need to think before we speak and think before we repeat stories that others have told us.

Who can spell "Think?"

T stands for True.  Think: Is the story true?

H is for helpful.  Is the story helpful?

I is for important.  Is the story important?

N is for necessary.  Is the story necessary?

K is for kind.  Is the story kind?

God wants you to think before you start a story or repeat a story that may hurt someone else.

How many want Jesus to help them speak good words that build others up, not tear them down?

title: Freedom Isn't Free
theme: Memorial Day reminds us of those who died for our freedoms; Jesus died for our freedom from sin.
object: American flag. Pictures of Memorial day.
body:
This weekend is a special weekend.  Does anyone know why?  What holiday do we celebrate?

Who knows what Memorial Day is all about?  Why do we celebrate Memorial Day?

The word Memorial is very close to another word we use: memory.  Memorial Day is a day to remember something.

Memorial Day is a day to remember all the soldiers that fought and died for our country.

Unfortunately, there are evil people in the world who try to take over other countries.

And when these evil people take over, they take away our freedoms and make us live according to their rules.

So we have to send our army to fight to keep our country free and independent.

Some of the soldiers that fight to keep our country free get killed by the enemy.

So on Memorial Day we remember all those soldiers that died to keep our country free.

On Memorial Day we have parades and speeches and patriotic music.

And people proudly wave what I brought in my bag today.  Why would they wave this flag?  What country does it stand for?

This flag is a symbol that reminds us of the great country in which we live.

This flag is a symbol that reminds us of the freedoms we enjoy: the freedom of worship, the freedom of speech, the freedom of assembly, the freedom to elect our own government.

And on Memorial Day this flag is a symbol that reminds us of those who died to protect these freedoms.

If you have a flag of the USA, I hope you'll fly it Monday, May 30, Memorial Day.

Here are some other pictures that remind us of what Memorial Day means.  [Show pictures.  End with the one that says Freedom isn't Free.]

Many people died so that we could live in a free country and be our own boss.

But the greatest freedom we can enjoy is the freedom that Jesus gives us.

Remember our Bible text?  If Jesus makes us free, we shall be free indeed.

The freedom that Jesus gives us is the freedom from sin.

He frees us from the penalty of sin, from the power of sin, and, when He comes again, He will free us from the presence of sin.

But the freedom that Jesus gives us isn't free either.

He had to die for our sins so that we could be forgiven.

The penalty for sin is death, and because we have all sinned we all deserve death.

But Jesus took the penalty for us: He died for our sins on the cross.

So if we love Jesus and trust in Him and tell Him we're sorry for our sins and ask Him to forgive us…

He will forgive us and give us eternal life instead of eternal death.

Is that Good News?!!  Jesus sets us free from sin.

Let's ask for that freedom today, what do you say?
